title: LHF Connect
author: Catalano, Manuel Catalano
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by
description:
Robot Avatar for Telepresence. Useful for connecting patients in isolation with relatives or doctors, via video call. 
The robot is operated remotely by a volunteer who manages movements and connections. The patient can carry out private conversations respecting his privacy 

 LHF-Connect connects people who are in isolation, in hospital or in quarantine, with their loved ones or medical consultants without them having to physically approach them. It can also carry small items and monitor the environment. 

 LHF-Connect is a tool that can be created with limited costs (around € 1200) using technologies that are widespread and available online in a few days. 

 No special technical skills are required to make an LHF-Connect robot. The project is open source, and the software is easy to download. 

 The name LHF indicates the acronym Low Hanging Fruits, i.e. those fruits of the most advanced robotic research carried out in past years, which are now at hand for a vast and immediate application. 

 The initiative was created to respond to the Covid-19 emergency, in a broad coalition of the actors described in this image.
